sentences.text: I have three years of experience in software development.
sentences.expected: i have three years of experience in software development
sentences.hint: Say the TH in 'three' by touching your tongue to your teeth, and stress the second syllable of 'experience'

sentences.text: My greatest strength is my ability to work under pressure.
sentences.expected: my greatest strength is my ability to work under pressure
sentences.hint: Focus on the TH in 'strength', the R in 'work', and the reduced vowel in 'pressure'

sentences.text: I am very enthusiastic about this opportunity.
sentences.expected: i am very enthusiastic about this opportunity
sentences.hint: Stress the third syllable of 'enthusiastic' and keep the TH clear in 'this'

sentences.text: I believe I would be a valuable addition to your team.
sentences.expected: i believe i would be a valuable addition to your team
sentences.hint: Stress 'VAL-u-able' and 'a-DI-tion' correctly

sentences.text: I led a team of fifteen engineers through a major product launch.
sentences.expected: i led a team of fifteen engineers through a major product launch
sentences.hint: Stress 'fif-TEEN', 'en-gi-NEERS', and soften the TH in 'through'

sentences.text: I am comfortable working both independently and collaboratively.
sentences.expected: i am comfortable working both independently and collaboratively
sentences.hint: Reduce the middle syllable of 'comfortable' and keep TH clear in 'both'

sentences.text: Where do you see yourself in the next five years?
sentences.expected: where do you see yourself in the next five years
sentences.hint: Practice the R sound clearly in 'where', 'yourself', and 'years'

sentences.text: I developed a process that reduced our delivery time by thirty percent.
sentences.expected: i developed a process that reduced our delivery time by thirty percent
sentences.hint: Clear TH in 'that' and 'thirty', and stress 'de-LIV-er-y' correctly

sentences.text: I thrive in fast-paced environments and enjoy learning new things.
sentences.expected: i thrive in fast-paced environments and enjoy learning new things
sentences.hint: Both 'thrive' and 'things' need a clear TH — touch your tongue to your teeth

sentences.text: Thank you for taking the time to meet with me today.
sentences.expected: thank you for taking the time to meet with me today
sentences.hint: Start 'thank' with a TH, use a soft T in 'taking', and end 'with' clearly

sentences.text: Let me walk you through the quarterly results.
sentences.expected: let me walk you through the quarterly results
sentences.hint: Make L clear in 'walk', say TH in 'through', and reduce the unstressed syllable in 'quarterly'

sentences.text: Today I would like to present our strategy for the coming year.
sentences.expected: today i would like to present our strategy for the coming year
sentences.hint: Stress 'pre-ZENT' as a verb, 'STRA-te-gy' with stress on first syllable, and clearly say the R in 'year'

sentences.text: This brings me to the third point of my presentation.
sentences.expected: this brings me to the third point of my presentation
sentences.hint: Clear TH in 'this' and 'third', and stress 'pre-zen-TAY-tion'

sentences.text: Are there any questions before I move on to the next section?
sentences.expected: are there any questions before i move on to the next section
sentences.hint: Use voiced TH in 'there' and stress 'QUES-tions' and 'SEC-tion'

sentences.text: The data clearly shows a significant increase in customer satisfaction.
sentences.expected: the data clearly shows a significant increase in customer satisfaction
sentences.hint: Flap the T in 'data', make the L clear in 'clearly', and stress 'sig-NIF-i-cant'

sentences.text: I would like to thank everyone for attending this afternoon.
sentences.expected: i would like to thank everyone for attending this afternoon
sentences.hint: Clear TH in 'thank' and 'this', and reduce the second syllable of 'everyone'

sentences.text: In conclusion, we need to rethink our approach to this problem.
sentences.expected: in conclusion we need to rethink our approach to this problem
sentences.hint: Stress 'con-CLU-sion', say TH in 'rethink' clearly, and voiced TH in 'this'

sentences.text: The research indicates that we should focus on three core areas.
sentences.expected: the research indicates that we should focus on three core areas
sentences.hint: Stress 'RE-search', clear TH in 'that' and 'three', and reduce unstressed vowels in 'indicates'

sentences.text: Please refer to the handout as we go through these slides.
sentences.expected: please refer to the handout as we go through these slides
sentences.hint: Distinguish R and L carefully in 'please' and 'refer', and use TH in 'through' and 'these'

sentences.text: Moving forward we will be implementing these changes in phases.
sentences.expected: moving forward we will be implementing these changes in phases
sentences.hint: Clear R in 'forward', stress 'IM-ple-ment-ing', and voiced TH in 'these'

sentences.text: I completely understand your frustration with this issue.
sentences.expected: i completely understand your frustration with this issue
sentences.hint: Stress 'com-PLETE-ly', clear TH in 'with' and 'this', and say the R in 'frustration'

sentences.text: Let me transfer you to the right department.
sentences.expected: let me transfer you to the right department
sentences.hint: Clear R in 'transfer' and 'right', and stress 'de-PART-ment'

sentences.text: Thank you for your patience while I look into this.
sentences.expected: thank you for your patience while i look into this
sentences.hint: TH in 'thank' and 'this', and stress 'PAY-tience'

sentences.text: I will escalate this to our technical support team right away.
sentences.expected: i will escalate this to our technical support team right away
sentences.hint: Stress 'ES-ca-late', clear TH in 'this', and 'TECH-ni-cal' with R in 'right'

sentences.text: I have verified your account and I can see the problem.
sentences.expected: i have verified your account and i can see the problem
sentences.hint: V sound in 'verified' (not W), stress 'a-COUNT', and clear R in 'problem'

sentences.text: We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience this has caused.
sentences.expected: we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience this has caused
sentences.hint: Clear R and L in 'sincerely', stress 'a-POL-o-gize', and 'in-con-VEN-ience'

sentences.text: Your refund will be processed within three to five business days.
sentences.expected: your refund will be processed within three to five business days
sentences.hint: Clear R in 'refund', TH in 'within' and 'three', and reduce the second syllable of 'business'

sentences.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today?
sentences.expected: is there anything else i can help you with today
sentences.hint: Practice TH in 'there', 'anything', and 'with' — all three need the tongue between your teeth

sentences.text: I will follow up with you within twenty-four hours.
sentences.expected: i will follow up with you within twenty-four hours
sentences.hint: Clear L in 'follow', and TH in 'with' and 'within'

sentences.text: I understand that this must be very frustrating for you.
sentences.expected: i understand that this must be very frustrating for you
sentences.hint: Clear TH in 'that' and 'this', and stress 'frus-TRAT-ing'

sentences.text: This solution will reduce your operational costs by thirty percent.
sentences.expected: this solution will reduce your operational costs by thirty percent
sentences.hint: TH in 'this' and 'thirty', stress 'op-er-A-tion-al', and flap the T in 'thirty'

sentences.text: We have helped over three hundred companies achieve their revenue goals.
sentences.expected: we have helped over three hundred companies achieve their revenue goals
sentences.hint: TH in 'three' and 'their', and stress 'REV-e-nue'

sentences.text: Our proven track record speaks for itself.
sentences.expected: our proven track record speaks for itself
sentences.hint: Clear final K in 'track', stress 'RE-cord' as a noun, and 'it-SELF'

sentences.text: Our platform is trusted by leading organizations worldwide.
sentences.expected: our platform is trusted by leading organizations worldwide
sentences.hint: R and L in 'platform', flap the T in 'trusted', and stress 'or-gan-i-ZA-tions'

sentences.text: Let me show you the return on investment you can expect.
sentences.expected: let me show you the return on investment you can expect
sentences.hint: Voiced TH in 'the', clear R in 'return', and stress 'in-VEST-ment'

sentences.text: I would love to schedule a quick thirty-minute demo for your team.
sentences.expected: i would love to schedule a quick thirty-minute demo for your team
sentences.hint: V sound in 'love', stress 'SCHED-ule', and TH in 'thirty'

sentences.text: With our solution your team will save at least four hours per week.
sentences.expected: with our solution your team will save at least four hours per week
sentences.hint: TH at the end of 'with', clear V in 'save', and L at the start of 'least'

sentences.text: Think of this as an investment in the future of your business.
sentences.expected: think of this as an investment in the future of your business
sentences.hint: TH in 'think' and 'this', and reduce the second syllable of 'future'

sentences.text: Other clients have reported a forty percent improvement in productivity.
sentences.expected: other clients have reported a forty percent improvement in productivity
sentences.hint: TH in 'other', flap the T in 'forty', stress 'im-PROVE-ment' and 'pro-duc-TIV-i-ty'

sentences.text: We would be delighted to offer you a special introductory rate.
sentences.expected: we would be delighted to offer you a special introductory rate
sentences.hint: Stress 'de-LIGHT-ed', R at end of 'offer', and 'in-tro-DUC-to-ry'

sentences.text: The weather has been really beautiful this week.
sentences.expected: the weather has been really beautiful this week
sentences.hint: TH in 'weather' and 'this', clear R and L in 'really', and stress 'BEAU-ti-ful'

sentences.text: It was wonderful to run into you the other day.
sentences.expected: it was wonderful to run into you the other day
sentences.hint: Stress 'WON-der-ful', clear R in 'run', and TH in 'other'

sentences.text: Would you like to grab a coffee sometime this week?
sentences.expected: would you like to grab a coffee sometime this week
sentences.hint: Clear L in 'like', stress 'COF-fee', and TH in 'this'

sentences.text: I heard that the restaurant on Third Avenue is really good.
sentences.expected: i heard that the restaurant on third avenue is really good
sentences.hint: TH in 'that' and 'third', stress 'RES-tau-rant', and clear R and L in 'really'

sentences.text: How are things going with your new place?
sentences.expected: how are things going with your new place
sentences.hint: TH in 'things' and 'with', and clear L in 'place'

sentences.text: We should definitely get together for dinner very soon.
sentences.expected: we should definitely get together for dinner very soon
sentences.hint: Stress 'DEF-i-nite-ly', TH in 'together', R in 'dinner', and clear V in 'very'

sentences.text: I really appreciated everything you did to help out.
sentences.expected: i really appreciated everything you did to help out
sentences.hint: R and L in 'really', stress 'ap-PRE-ci-a-ted', and TH in 'everything'

sentences.text: Have you thought about traveling anywhere interesting this summer?
sentences.expected: have you thought about traveling anywhere interesting this summer
sentences.hint: TH in 'thought' and 'this', stress 'TRAV-el-ing', and reduce the third syllable of 'interesting'

sentences.text: I can't believe how quickly the years have flown by.
sentences.expected: i can't believe how quickly the years have flown by
sentences.hint: Clear L and V in 'believe', and precise R and L distinction in 'quickly', 'years', and 'flown'

sentences.text: The neighborhood has really changed over the last few years.
sentences.expected: the neighborhood has really changed over the last few years
sentences.hint: R and L distinction throughout: 'NEIGH-bor-hood', 'RE-al-ly', and 'years'

sentences.text: I noticed you mentioned working in the healthcare industry before.
sentences.expected: i noticed you mentioned working in the healthcare industry before
sentences.hint: Flap the T in 'noticed', reduce unstressed vowels in 'mentioned', and clear R in 'healthcare'

sentences.text: I can assure you that we will resolve this within the hour.
sentences.expected: i can assure you that we will resolve this within the hour
sentences.hint: Stress 'as-SURE', TH in 'that', 'this', and 'within', and clear R in 'resolve'

sentences.text: Could you please provide your order number for verification?
sentences.expected: could you please provide your order number for verification
sentences.hint: Clear L in 'please', V in 'provide', R in 'order', and stress 'ver-i-fi-CA-tion'

sentences.text: I would like to highlight three key benefits of our product.
sentences.expected: i would like to highlight three key benefits of our product
sentences.hint: Clear L in 'like', TH in 'three', stress 'HIGH-light' and 'BEN-e-fits'

sentences.text: Could you walk me through your biggest operational challenge right now?
sentences.expected: could you walk me through your biggest operational challenge right now
sentences.hint: L in 'walk', TH in 'through', stress 'op-er-A-tion-al', and R in 'right'

sentences.text: I appreciate you taking the time to speak with me.
sentences.expected: i appreciate you taking the time to speak with me
sentences.hint: Stress 'ap-PRE-ci-ate', flap the T in 'taking', and TH at end of 'with'

sentences.text: I am particularly proud of the results I delivered in that role.
sentences.expected: i am particularly proud of the results i delivered in that role
sentences.hint: Stress 'par-TIC-u-lar-ly', clear R and L in 'results' and 'delivered', and TH in 'that'

sentences.text: I feel very comfortable presenting to large audiences.
sentences.expected: i feel very comfortable presenting to large audiences
sentences.hint: Clear L in 'feel', V in 'very', reduce syllables in 'comfortable', and stress 'pre-SENT-ing'

sentences.text: Before we wrap up I want to quickly recap the three action items.
sentences.expected: before we wrap up i want to quickly recap the three action items
sentences.hint: Clear R and L in 'quickly', TH in 'three', and stress 'RE-cap' as a verb
